build_check.description: The project launches with the headless command, exits with code 0, and produces no fatal errors. Returns 0 or 1; the rest of the score is multiplied by this gate.

requirements.description: The player rolls a set of dice (at least 5) each combat turn with a tumble animation. Die faces show ability icons (sword, shield, heart, skull, etc.). Results land face-up and are clearly readable. Score 0 if no dice rolling exists. Score 0.5 at most if dice exist but have no animation or faces are not readable. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

After rolling, the player selects dice to keep (they lock in place) and rerolls the rest — up to 2 rerolls per turn. The keep/reroll decision is the core tactical choice. Score 0 if no reroll mechanic exists. Score 0.5 at most if rerolling exists but is unlimited or has no lock mechanic. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

After finalizing dice, faces activate: swords deal damage, shields block incoming damage, hearts heal, skulls trigger special abilities. Each face type has a distinct and visible effect. Score 0 if dice faces have no gameplay effect. Score 0.5 at most if effects exist but fewer than 4 face types are functional. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

The enemy rolls visible dice with their own faces and resolves them similarly. Both player and enemy dice results are shown simultaneously, creating a transparent contest. Score 0 if enemies do not use dice or their rolls are hidden. Score 0.5 at most if enemy dice exist but are not visible before resolution. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Equipment found between encounters modifies die faces — replacing, upgrading, or adding faces to existing dice. A flame sword replaces a sword face with a fire-sword that deals bonus damage. At least 3 face-modifying items exist. Score 0 if no equipment or face modification exists. Score 0.5 at most if equipment exists but does not modify die faces. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

At least 6 distinct enemy types with different dice configurations — some have more attack faces, some have shields, some have special abilities. Different enemies require different keep/reroll strategies. Score 0 if fewer than 3 enemy types exist. Score 0.5 at most if enemies exist but all have identical dice. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

At least 8 distinct equipment items exist that modify die faces in different ways (elemental upgrades, defensive conversions, special ability additions). Equipment creates build diversity. Score 0 if fewer than 4 items exist. Score 0.5 at most if items exist but all modify faces identically. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

A map shows branching paths with combat, elite, shop, and rest nodes. Shops sell face modifications and new dice. The player chooses their route. Score 0 if no map or path choice exists. Score 0.5 at most if a map exists but does not branch. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

A boss encounter with powerful custom dice caps the run. The boss has unique face types not seen on regular enemies and requires an optimized dice build to defeat. Score 0 if no boss exists. Score 0.5 at most if a boss exists but uses standard dice. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

A death/victory screen shows floor reached, best single roll (most damage in one turn), equipment collected, and total encounters won. Score 0 if no results exist. Score 0.5 at most if only basic info is shown. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

A styled title screen shows dice tumbling with glowing face icons and the game name. Score 0 if the game starts directly in gameplay or uses raw default UI. Score 0.5 at most if a title exists but is sparse. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

The game flow connects title -> map -> combat (roll/keep/resolve) -> loot -> next node without restarting. Score 0 if transitions are missing. Score 0.5 at most if flow exists but is broken. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

During combat, player dice, enemy dice, health bars, reroll counter, and face effect resolution are all clearly visible and non-overlapping. Score 0 if no combat display exists. Score 0.5 at most if displays are incomplete. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Readability at 1280x720 is strong: die faces are large and legible, face types are visually distinct, locked vs unlocked dice are clear, and enemy dice are readable. Score 0 if dice are too small or faces indistinguishable. Score 0.5 at most if visuals are dominated by raw shapes. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

The art direction is coherent across title, map, combat, dice, equipment, and UI — a consistent fantasy dice-combat aesthetic with bold iconography. Score 0 if mostly default UI or mismatched placeholders dominate. Score 0.5 at most if visuals are sparse or generic. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Dice have polished 3D-style or illustrated faces with distinct icons per type. Enemies have character sprites. Equipment items have illustrated icons. Score 0 if dice are plain numbered squares. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

HUD and UI chrome: health bars, reroll counter, map interface, shop screens, and loot displays use styled themed widgets — not raw unstyled Godot defaults. Score 0 if primary UI elements are obviously unstyled. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Animation and feedback: dice tumble with physics animation, locked dice glow, face activation produces effect particles (sword slash, shield flash, heal sparkle), and damage numbers float. Score 0 if all state changes snap silently. Score 0.5 at most if animation is minimal. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.
